original_image_tags: A handwritten letter on "HOTEL WOODSTOCK, WOODSTOCK, ILLINOIS" letterhead. The letter is addressed to "Dr. Mr. Hoover" and concerns "Flying Sauces". It is stamped "G.I.R. - 5". The bottom of the letter has a "RECEIVED FILED" stamp, the number "54", and the date "JUL 29 1947". There are various handwritten notes in the margins and several small hole punches at the top of the page. A red stamp on the left side reads "ANONYMOUS COMMUNICATIONS KEEP ENVELOPE ATTACHED".

elements.description: A page consisting of a typed hotel letterhead, a large block of handwritten text, and several stamps. The letterhead reads 'HOTEL WOODSTOCK WOODSTOCK, ILLINOIS' and 'PHONE 606 BOOTH PHO 814 LOCATED IN BUSINESS DISTRICT'. There is a blue handwritten note 'Flying Saucers' at the top right. A prominent red stamp reads 'RECEIVED JUL 29 1947'. Other visible stamps include 'G.I.R.-5', 'NONMOTORS COMMUNICATIONS DEEP ENVELOPE ATTACHED', and various partially legible numerical stamps and annotations in blue, red, and pencil ink across the page. No photographs, sketches, or diagrams are present.

judge.reason: The two descriptions give substantially different verbatim text for the same stamp ('ANONYMOUS COMMUNICATIONS KEEP ENVELOPE ATTACHED' vs. 'NONMOTORS COMMUNICATIONS DEEP ENVELOPE ATTACHED'), indicating at least one side is hallucinating the exact text of the stamp.
